meta.note: Mixed: kn-s05/kn-s06/kn-s08/kn-s09 are real working knowledge; the rest are demo/draft records flagged accordingly.

items.title: Voice bible pointer
items.body: The full voice document lives at data/voice/stuart-voice.md. The linter enforces the mechanical rules; the bible carries the judgement.

items.title: POV: the CRM is never the problem
items.body: Current view: CRM migrations fail because lifecycle definitions were never agreed. Confidence: high, from repeated direct observation. Counterargument: sometimes the tool genuinely cannot model the business; rare below 100 staff. Related: ins-s01, cnt-s01.

items.title: POV: sell clarity, not hours
items.body: Strait Up Growth sells commercial clarity and operating leverage. Day rates commoditise judgement. Confidence: medium; pricing model untested. DRAFT ASSUMPTION.

items.title: Objection: "we already have consultants"
items.body: Response angle: consultants produce decks; this produces an operating system the team runs weekly. Needs proof point before use. [unverified]

items.title: NEXT.io confidentiality boundary
items.body: Deals, margins, pipeline, people and unannounced strategy from NEXT.io never enter public or Strait Up Growth material. Roles and counts, never names or figures. Mirrors next-os rule R8.

items.title: Proof points available (career, public CV)
items.body: Built NEXT.io media division to €1.2M baseline (3x in 18 months); ACV €60K to €100K+; €500K+ monthly affiliate revenue engine; APAC P&Ls at Microgaming and W.Media; prediction-markets vertical GTM. Use bucketed versions publicly; exact figures only where already public in the CV.

items.title: Singapore SME AI adoption pattern
items.body: Founders buy pain relief, not models (ins-s10). Adoption stalls without workflow mapping (ins-s04). Two live insights support this; keep collecting.

items.title: Outreach shape that works
items.body: Real reason for writing now, one line. Value to them, concrete. One low-pressure ask. Under 150 words. No fake familiarity, no biography.

items.title: Frequently used links
items.body: CV: stuart-crowley-cv repo. NEXTPredict summit page: see nextpredict-engine gtm.mjs. LinkedIn: linkedin.com/in/stuart-crowley-b2b561104.

items.title: Tanjong founder session notes (fictional)
items.body: Talk on operating drag landed; strongest reaction to the "waiting on founder" CRM stage line. Devi Menon asked for the checklist. Follow-up captured as eng/tsk records.

items.title: Angle: the contrarian read
items.body: Take the consensus reaction to the news and argue the operator's opposite: "everyone is reading this as X; from inside a commercial team it looks like Y". Only when genuinely held.

items.title: Angle: cost-of-inaction arithmetic
items.body: Put rough, honest numbers on doing nothing: what the unowned follow-up, the informal discount, the untrusted CRM costs per quarter. Bucketed figures, never invented precision.

items.title: Angle: the public teardown
items.body: Take a public example (a pricing page, a sponsorship deck, an event website) and read it like an operator. Punch at patterns, never at people or small businesses.

items.title: Angle: build-in-public diary
items.body: Strait Up Growth in Asia as an honest running story: what was tried, what worked, what did not. Uncertainty is the feature; it separates Stuart from the guru crowd.

items.title: Angle: the diagnostic checklist
items.body: Turn a lesson into 5 questions a founder can score themselves against. The audience self-diagnoses, which is what converts readers into conversations.

items.title: Angle: the dated prediction
items.body: A specific, falsifiable call with a date, marked [prediction]. Revisit publicly when the date arrives, right or wrong; the revisit is a second post and compounding credibility.

items.title: Angle: reader question, real answer
items.body: Take an actual question from comments/DMs (with permission or anonymised) and answer it properly. Signals the room is alive and generates more questions.

items.title: Angle: what I got wrong
items.body: A decision Stuart would take differently now, and the operating rule that came out of it. The highest-trust format there is; use sparingly so it stays honest.

items.title: Angle: the cross-industry transplant
items.body: Take a mechanism from one lane (iGaming supplier pricing, event sponsorship renewals, prediction-market compliance hiring) and show what another industry can steal from it. Stuart's unfair advantage is having operated in several.

items.title: Angle: the Asia lens
items.body: The same commercial pattern read from Singapore/SEA: what Western B2B advice gets wrong locally (payments, trust, relationship pace, channel mix). Underserved and exactly the SUG ICP.
